title: Technical Problem Solving, Coding, and Work-Sample Boundaries · Claim Audit: Correctness First
summary: A timed exercise rewards silent pattern recall while the candidate ignores requirements, changed cases, testing, communication, accessibility, security, tool policy, and task proportionality. Predict the hiring decision, evidence quality, affected people, uncertainty, and ethical boundary before reviewing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the correctness first increment in observable technical interview practice system. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Build the smallest correct behavior, test examples and changed cases, inspect failures, then improve measured cost or design.
Externalize assumptions, alternatives, invariants, tradeoffs, checkpoints, uncertainty, and help requests without narrating every keystroke.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Predict Correctness First · Theory Correctness First
steps.instruction:
theory case — A timed task begins without contract or tool rules, optimizes before correctness, tests only the sample, and quietly resembles unpaid production work. The theory variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Correctness First contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Correctness First · Theory Correctness First.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for build the smallest correct behavior, test examples and changed cases, inspect failures, then improve measured cost or design. For Predict Correctness First · Theory Correctness First, keep Correctness First connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A timed task begins without contract or tool rules, optimizes before correctness, tests only the sample, and quietly resembles unpaid production work. content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A timed task begins without contract or tool rules, optimizes before correctness, tests only the sample, and quietly resembles unpaid production work. The worked example variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Build the smallest correct behavior, test examples and changed cases, inspect failures, then improve measured cost or design. A defensible solution applies Correctness First, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to observable technical interview practice system.

content.code:
# Worked focus: career-correctness-first — Build the smallest correct behavior, test examples and changed cases, inspect failures, then improve measured cost or design.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-candidate-5
focus: career-correctness-first
decision-evidence: Build the smallest correct behavior, test examples and changed cases, inspect failures, then improve measured cost or design.
target-role: accessible software quality engineer
jurisdiction: example location requiring current official review
audience: technical reviewer with ten minutes and keyboard-only access
claim: one bounded role behavior is supported by a revision-linked changed case
evidence: repository case study, exact test result, contribution note, and limitation
process-state: investigate before applying
accessibility: structured text, reflow, visible focus, plain-language status, and equivalent format
privacy-consent: publish no sensitive person or third-party data; contact only with explicit consent
decision: proceed only after identity, role, evidence, access, and risk checks pass
verify: inspect links, extraction order, revision, changed case, disclosure, and source date
failure: reject invented impact, hidden assistance, exposed data, or unverified recruiter identity
repair: correct the first unsupported claim and add a changed-candidate regression
owner: learner controls every external action
transfer-boundary: manual learner-approved profile, application, conversation, interview, and offer decisions
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: The optimal algorithm matters before the contract works. This fails the Correctness First contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: CareerOneStop Job Search (U.S. Department of Labor sponsored guidance reviewed 2026-07-15) — Controls the connected search flow across planning, employer research, networking, applications, portfolios, interviews, background checks, and negotiation.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build the smallest correct behavior, test examples and changed cases, inspect failures, then improve measured cost or design.
